term: Mixed question of law and fact
definition: A question that requires applying a legal standard to a set of facts. It is neither purely legal (what does the law say?) nor purely factual (what happened?) but involves both — determining whether what happened satisfies what the law requires.
examples: Whether the threats Urias-Orellana experienced constitute 'persecution' under the INA is a mixed question requiring application of a legal term to undisputed facts
